g.text: predicted velocity v (16, F', H/16, W/16)
patchify → 30 DiT blocks (+ VACE hints) → head → unpatchify (figs 2–11)
noised latent x_σ (16, F', H/16, W/16)
Wan2.1 VAE: a video (3, F, H, W) becomes a latent (16,
noise (σ=1) and Euler-integrates v down to σ=0; the VAE maps pixels ↔ latents at the two ends.

The DiT is a function v = f(x_σ, σ, text, control): IN a noised latent x_σ at level σ, OUT a velocity of the SAME shape. Training regresses v to ε − x_0 on the straight path x_σ = (1−σ)x_0 + σε. Sampling starts at
Wan2.1-VACE-1.3B · 1 · the model is a velocity field; sampling is a flow-matching loop
